justification: All pass criteria met: Breaking change clearly identified as cardinality shift from 1:1 to 1:N. New minimum-cardinality invariant stated (BR-1: >= 1 Recipient). Data migration impact for ~2M records noted in business_impact and BR-4. External API breakage identified in BR-5 and edge cases. 'Delivery preferences' ambiguity surfaced as first escalation. Architectural concerns flagged (migration strategy, API versioning). Confidence level 52, well under 75 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough analysis — went beyond rubric requirements with per-recipient delivery status tracking (BR-6), duplicate address invariant (BR-7), and pricing model escalation
Correctly classified invariants vs. policies (e.g., BR-4 migration as non-invariant, BR-1 minimum cardinality as invariant)
Strong edge cases including lazy vs. batch migration timing, mid-delivery updates, and refused delivery handling
Confidence level of 52 reflects genuine uncertainty with well-articulated high/low confidence areas
Identified additional ambiguities beyond rubric expectations: upper bound on recipients, pricing model, migration strategy
Did not explicitly name MJ for architectural migration plan — escalations exist but lack agent routing
External API backward compatibility addressed with concrete v1/v2 versioning strategy in BR-5 and AC-4
